preFix.title: Key lime pie
preFix.subtitle: Tart, creamy American pie with a biscuit base
preFix.excerpt: Key lime pie is a cold-set American custard pie made from condensed milk, lime juice, and egg yolks in a digestive biscuit crust. The filling is sharpened by the acid from the limes, which partially sets the condensed milk without cooking. It bakes briefly to finish the set, then chills before serving. The flavour is clean, sharp, and rich.
preFix.sourceNotes: Key lime pie is documented as a Floridian specialty from at least the 1930s, associated with the Florida Keys where small, tart Key limes (Citrus aurantiifolia) were abundant. The combination of condensed milk with lime juice sets by acid reaction and was noted as a method requiring no cooking before refrigeration was common in the region. Standard Persian limes (the common supermarket variety) can be substituted with the same result. The digestive biscuit base is a British adaptation of the original cracker crust.

content.text: The filling for key lime pie sets primarily through the chemical reaction between the lime juice and the condensed milk, but a brief bake gives it a more stable set and slightly less raw flavour. The biscuit base is pressed by hand rather than blind-baked dry; it just needs 10 minutes in the oven to firm up before the filling goes in. Use fresh limes: the bottled juice lacks the volatile oils from the zest and the flavour is flatter.

items.name: Double cream
items.prepNote: whipped to soft peaks, to serve

content.text: Heat the oven to 180 degrees C fan. Mix 200 g crushed digestive biscuits with 75 g melted butter and 2 tablespoons of caster sugar. Press firmly and evenly into the base and up the sides of a 23 cm loose-bottomed or springform tin. Bake for 10 minutes until set and smelling toasted. Reduce the oven to 160 degrees C fan.

In a medium bowl, whisk together 400 g condensed milk, 3 egg yolks, the zest of all 5 limes, and 120 ml freshly squeezed lime juice until smooth and thickened slightly: the acid will begin to set the mixture immediately. Pour into the warm base and bake at 160 degrees C fan for 12 to 15 minutes until the filling is set at the edges but still has a slight wobble in the centre when shaken. Remove and cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 3 hours.

items.problem: The filling did not set and is liquid after chilling
items.solution: The pie needed more time in the oven. The filling should not wobble freely when shaken: a very slight, central wobble only. Return to the oven for a further 5 minutes.

The base was not pressed firmly enough or not prebaked long enough. Press it with the back of a spoon until compacted to 5 mm even thickness, and make sure it has had 10 full minutes in the oven before the filling goes in.
